mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation indicates capacity utilization improvements without pricing pressure, supporting equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth, or 20-day forward returns show no positive alpha in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 2.0% combined with rapid tightening triggers liquidity concerns that compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns remain positive in over 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the negative correlation vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth alongside stable unemployment rates reflects healthy job creation without wage inflation pressures, sustaining corporate profit margins and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when UNRATE rises concurrently with PAYEMS, or the interaction effect yields a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal rising residential investment and construction sector activity that flows through to broader economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the signal generates negative alpha in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during high unemployment indicates a demand-driven recessionary shock that forces earnings downgrades, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns do not materialize in over 55% of cases when INDPRO falls during high unemployment regimes, or predictive accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.
